summary.what: Gate post-commit hook on actionable pending work
summary.why: Pending, log, and the post-commit hook must agree on what counts as undocumented — divergence creates contradictory signals for agents (hook nudges to log a commit that timbers log refuses)
summary.how: Extracted hasActionablePending() helper that combines IsRepo, IsInteractiveGitOp, .timbers/ existence, storage construction, and HasPendingCommits checks; both pre-commit and post-commit hooks now route through it so they share one definition of actionable. Added cmd/timbers/hook_run_test.go with table-driven cases for .beads/, .timbers/, lockfile, .timbersignore-skipped, and substantive commits plus a missing-.timbers/ case and a pre-commit parity test.

notes: Considered narrower fix (inline the HasPendingCommits gate in runPostCommitHook only) but the helper extraction eliminates the divergence root cause — anyone adding a third hook later (post-rewrite?) gets the same gate for free, and the parity test reads as one assertion. The test harness needed a 'seedFile' escape hatch so .timbersignore can be baked into the initial commit; otherwise adding it as a separate commit makes IT actionable and the skipped-vendor commit comes after pending=true. Real bug found in gorewood/vellum where .beads/issues.jsonl-only commits triggered the nudge; root cause was runPostCommitHook never checking HasPendingCommits before printing.
